extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT attacked solo early, established breakaway, conquered Mont Ventoux twice, and won by holding lead on final descent to Malaucène.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE attacked category-4 climb solo to establish breakaway, prioritizing descent opportunity over summit finish tactics.
VAN AERT sustained solo effort over double-Ventoux circuit while POGAČAR remained passive until final descent chase.
ELISSONDE launched Trek duo attack but BALLERINI teammate orders yielded second place, revealing team hierarchy dynamics.
VINGEGAARD briefly distanced POGAČAR on final Ventoux ascent, first visible weakness shown by yellow jersey.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: dominated double-Ventoux queen stage as sprinter, suggesting exceptional climbing form adaptation beyond typical capabilities
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: aggressive early solo move on cat-4 failed to establish position, suggesting poor breakaway selection judgment
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: visibly troubled chasing VINGEGAARD on final Ventoux ascent before recovering on descent
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: briefly dropped POGAČAR on final Ventoux climb, first rider to genuinely distance yellow jersey
rider_signals.ELISSONDE Kenny: positive: launched Trek attack and secured second after BALLERINI teammate concession
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: dropped from 2:00 to 5:58 behind yellow jersey, massive GC collapse on mountain stage
